description_long: This object consists of a set of five rectangular cabinets or shelves, each varying in length but sharing a similar height and depth. The two largest units feature open tops and visible internal shelving, with one of them containing a set of angled, sawtooth-like dividers or racks. The remaining three units are closed rectangular boxes, likely serving as storage cabinets. The surfaces are smooth and appear to be made of metal or painted wood, with a uniform gray color throughout. The design is utilitarian and modular, suitable for use in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or public spaces. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, with ample space for storing or organizing items.
description: A modular set of five gray rectangular cabinets or shelves, with two open units featuring internal racks and three closed storage boxes. The design is utilitarian, smooth, and suitable for urban storage or organization.
description_view_0: This view shows the front and top of the cabinets, highlighting the open tops and internal racks of the two largest units, as well as the closed nature of the three smaller units.
description_view_1: From this side view, the depth and stepped arrangement of the cabinets are visible, with the internal racks in the open units clearly seen.
description_view_2: This top-down view emphasizes the open compartments and internal dividers of the two largest units, while the three smaller units appear as solid rectangles.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the solid backs of all five units, with the open racks and shelving in the two largest units visible from the side.
